conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
That's good to hear. So, what brings you in today? What's been going on that's got you thinking about seeking help?
Well, I just feel really drained all the time. I mean, I'm a college student and it's supposed to be exciting, but honestly, I just can't seem to get out of bed.
Can you tell me more about what a typical day looks like for you? What are some things that you're struggling with?

I don't know. Everything feels so overwhelming. Schoolwork is piling up and I'm constantly worried about making ends meet financially. And on top of that, my friends are all moving on to different places after graduation, so it's like, what am I even doing here?
That sounds really tough. Have you noticed any changes in your sleep patterns lately?
Yeah, actually. I've been sleeping a lot more than usual. Like, sometimes I'll fall asleep in class or during TV shows and stuff.
I see. And what about energy levels? Do you feel like you're able to do the things you want to do?
Honestly, no. It's like, if I don't get enough sleep, I just crash. But even when I do get some rest, it doesn't seem to make a difference.
Okay, got it. And what about your mood? Are there any emotions that you're experiencing more frequently than others?
Yeah... I've been feeling really down lately. Like, sometimes I'll just start crying and can't stop for hours.
That sounds really tough to deal with. Have you noticed any patterns or triggers that might be contributing to those feelings?
Honestly, it's hard to say. Sometimes I think it's because of school stress, but other times it feels like there's just this general feeling of sadness in the air.
Okay, got it. And what about your relationships? Are you close with anyone right now?
Yeah, my mom is really supportive. She's always asking me how I'm doing and stuff.
That's great to hear. What about other friendships or social connections?
Eh, not so much. Everyone seems to have their own thing going on and I just feel like an outsider sometimes.
Okay, got it. And what are some things that you enjoy doing when you're feeling good? Any hobbies or activities that bring you joy?
Honestly, not really. Like, I used to love reading, but lately even that's been hard to get into.
That sounds tough. Have you noticed any changes in your concentration or ability to focus recently?
Yeah, definitely. It takes me a lot longer than usual to complete tasks and stuff.
Okay, got it. And have you experienced any physical sensations or body feelings that might be related to your emotional state?
Sometimes my hands get really cold and clammy, and I'll feel like I'm going to pass out if I don't sit down or something.
Okay, got it. Have there been any recent stressful events or changes in your life that might have triggered some of these feelings?
Well, school's just been a real struggle lately. And my mom has been dealing with some health issues so I've had to take on more responsibilities at home.
Okay, got it. What are some things that you hope to accomplish or achieve in our time together? Are there any specific goals or areas of focus that you'd like to work on?
Honestly, just being able to feel happy and fulfilled again. Like, I used to love my major and was really excited about college, but now it just feels like a chore.
Okay, got it. Well, we can definitely work towards that. What do you think might be some steps or strategies that could help us get there?
I don't know... maybe finding ways to manage my time better? Or learning how to say no to things that drain me?
Those are both great places to start. And what about seeking support from your mom or other loved ones? Have you talked to them about how you're feeling?
Yeah, they've been really supportive, but sometimes it's hard for them to understand because they can't see it.
Okay, got it. Well, we can definitely work on that too. What do you think might be some ways to help your mom or others understand what you're going through?
Maybe if I wrote her a letter? Or talked to her about how I'm feeling in a way that's more concrete and easy to grasp?

That sounds like a great idea! We can definitely work on finding ways for you to communicate effectively with your mom. And what about setting some small, achievable goals for yourself? What might be something you'd like to accomplish this week or next?
Honestly, just getting out of bed without wanting to crawl back in.

Well, that's a great place to start! We can work on building up your energy and motivation. And finally, are there any specific things that you hope to get out of our time together? What do you think you might need or want from me as your therapist?
Honestly, just someone who gets it. Someone who doesn't judge me for feeling this way.
